cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what can be learned from the text. The options are A to D. Let me go through the text again to make sure I understand the key points. The text starts by talking about Fox Point, a new building in South Bronx, which is one of the poorest areas in New York. The majority of the residents are formerly homeless people with government-paid rent, and the rest are low-income families. The boiler room there is clean and cool, using special equipment to produce energy efficiently, reusing heat to reduce carbon emissions and cut costs. Then it mentions Palladia and ECP, which help provide affordable housing with green standards. The Green Communities Standards include water conservation, energy saving, and eco-friendly materials. The cost is a bit higher (2%), but it's offset by lower running costs. Then the mayor, Michael Bloomberg, is planning to create 165,000 affordable units for 500,000 New Yorkers. The text states that almost 80% of the city's greenhouse gas emissions come from buildings, and 40% of those are from housing. So the DHPD will require all new projects to follow ECP's green standards. Other cities like Cleveland and Denver have similar measures, but NY's DHPD is the largest city developer of affordable housing in the country. Now
